role_summary: Functional specification defining user-centric requirements and acceptance criteria for an automated repository analysis and documentation pipeline. The file establishes the domain logic for translating source code into business-readable artifacts, supporting workflows for acquisition assessment, engineering onboarding, and technical documentation generation with strict provenance and auditability.
finding: The system is architected as a deterministic, four-stage processing pipeline that ingests source repositories to produce domain-centric documentation and behavioral models. Key functional capabilities include intelligent structural traversal with configurable filtering, granular extraction of business rules from technical code with immutable extraction notes, dual-mode generation of schema-validated and narrative content, and cross-component relationship mapping. The specification enforces high-fidelity governance by prohibiting speculative content, requiring explicit gap declarations for ambiguities, and ensuring full traceability from generated artifacts back to original source files. Significant architectural gaps are identified, including undefined heuristics for non-essential artifact classification, lack of strategies for reconciling contradictory insights, unspecified data serialization schemas for module handoffs, and missing security/access control definitions.
